mapping_definition.name: GRID-Raw-Equivalence-Map
mapping_definition.description: Precise field path definitions for the GRIDd join pipeline, based on the raw GRIDr schemas of BSI-CSAF and ENISA-EUVD documents.
notes: CSAF documents have the structure: { document: {...}, product_tree: {...}, vulnerabilities: [...] }
The 'vulnerabilities' array is at the TOP LEVEL of the stored document, NOT nested under 'document'.
EUVD 'aliases' is a raw newline-separated string. CVE extraction requires a regex: /CVE-\d{4}-\d+/g

primary_key.note: Extract CVE IDs from both sides and join if any CVE-ID is shared. A single CSAF advisory may cover multiple CVEs — each is checked against the EUVD aliases string.

cvss_base_score.note: CSAF may have multiple score objects per vulnerability. Take the first cvss_v3 entry.

summary_text.note: CSAF stores the summary inside a notes array filtered by category. EUVD stores it as a direct string.

title.note: EUVD has no advisory title. The EUVD-ID is the closest equivalent for display purposes.

severity_text.note: EUVD has no textual severity equivalent. Use baseScore thresholds to derive severity if needed.

exploitation_status.note: CSAF uses a threats array with category filtering. EUVD uses a direct 'exploitedSince' date field (camelCase, raw API name).

epss_score.note: EPSS is only available from EUVD. No CSAF equivalent.

references.note: CSAF: array of objects, filter by category='external', extract 'url'. EUVD: newline-separated string of URLs — split on '\n' to get array.

affected_os.note: Affected OS is only available in CSAF (notes with category='general'). No EUVD equivalent.
